text: Plates collide, pull apart, or slide — each creates unique hazards. These boundaries are alive with movement, shaping our planet. First, convergent boundaries: plates push together, causing intense earthquakes. Next, divergent boundaries: where plates pull apart, magma rises, creating new land. Finally, transform boundaries: plates slide past each other, often leading to tension. Understanding these interactions helps us predict hazards and protect lives. Curious to learn more about our planet? Hit that follow button!
